creator: makc@issp.ac.ru
text:
On Fri, 12 Mar 2010 17:50:12 -0500, Jon wrote:
> qtoctave was not installed contemporaneously with octave.  THere may have
> been port updates in between.  Could this cause problems?
Unlikely, however rebuilding ports never hurts :). Does octave crash when it 
runs outside qtoctave? If so, could you remove $HOME/.qtoctave and try again?
 
> Segmentation fault also reported for octave started from qtoctave in
> Fedora bug-list.
